Can Push-forward Generative Models Fit Multimodal Distributions ?

  • Antoine Salmona
  • Centre Borelli
  • ENS Paris Saclay, France
  • AgnĂ¨s Desolneux
  • Centre Borelli, CNRS
  • ENS Paris Saclay, France
  • Julie Delon
  • MAP5, UniversitĂ© Paris CitĂ©, France
  • Institut Universitaire de France (IUF)
  • Valentin De Bortoli
  • Center for Sciences of Data, CNRS
  • ENS Ulm, France

Plan

  1. Generative modeling
    • a. C'est quoi ?
    • b. Pourquoi c'est dur ?
    • pad
  2. Push-forward model
    • pad
  3. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique
    • a. Cas simple
    • b. Extension
    • pad
  4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models
    • a. PrĂ©sentation
    • b. ConsĂ©quences
    • pad

1. Generative Modeling

a. C'est quoi ?

  • Dataset : $\{x_1, \ldots, x_N\} \sim \nu(x)$
  • But : sampler de nouveaux Ă©chantillons de $\nu(x)$
  • pad

"An anthropomorphic fractal person behind the counter at a fractal themed restaurant."

1. Generative Modeling

b. Pourquoi c'est dur ?

pad

  • Estimateur paramĂ©trique $$\nu_{\theta}(x) = \frac{e^{-f_{\theta}(x)}}{Z_{\theta}}$$
  • Maximum de vraisemblance $$\max_{\theta} \sum_{i=1}^N \log \nu_{\theta}(x_i)$$

    $$\log \nu_{\theta}(x_i) = -f_{\theta}(x_i) - \textcolor{red}{\log Z_{\theta}}$$

2. Push-forward Generative Models

Transformer une distribution connue en notre distribution cible

  • Distribution prior, samplable : $\quad\mu_p = \mathcal{N}(0, \mathrm{Id}_p)$
  • Mapping dĂ©terministe, inversible : $g_{\theta}$ (rĂ©seau de neurones)
  • Sampling procedure : $$x \sim \mu_p\,,\quad y=g_{\theta}(x)$$
  • Loi de y :

    $$\nu(y) = \mu(g_{\theta}^{-1}(y)) \bigg\lvert \det{\frac{\partial g_{\theta}^{-1}}{\partial y}} (y) \bigg\rvert = \frac{\mu(x)}{\big\lvert \det{\frac{\partial g_{\theta}}{\partial x}} (x) \big\rvert}$$

    $$\nu_{\theta} = g_{\theta \#}\mu_p$$

  • VAE, GAN, NF
  • variational lower bound, minimax zero sum game Nash equilibrium, exact likelihood

3. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique

a. Forme basique

  • InĂ©galitĂ© entre pĂ©rimĂ¨tre et surface

pad

  • pad
  • $p^2 \geq 4 \pi S\quad\quad$ (Ă©galitĂ© = disque)
  • pad
  • $S^3 \geq 36 \pi V^2\quad$ (Ă©galitĂ© = sphĂ¨re)

3. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique

b. Extension Ă  des surfaces gĂ©nĂ©ralisĂ©es

pad

  • $(\mathcal{X}, d, \gamma)$ espace mesurĂ©e, $A$ borĂ©lien de $\mathcal{X}$ (Ă©vĂ¨nement)
  • r-voisinage (ouvert) de $A$ : $$A_r = \{ x \in \mathcal{X}, d(x, A) < r \}$$
  • "pĂ©rimĂ¨tre" de $A$ : $$\gamma^{+}(\partial A) = \underset{r \, \rightarrow \, 0}{\lim \inf} \; \frac{\gamma(A_r) - \gamma(A)}{r}$$
  • fonction isopĂ©rimĂ©trique $\mathcal{I}_{\gamma}$ de $\gamma$ : $$\gamma^{+}(\partial A) \geq \mathcal{I}_{\gamma}(\gamma(A))$$

3. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique

b. Extension Ă  des surfaces gĂ©nĂ©ralisĂ©es

  • Mesure gaussienne $\mu_p$ dans $\mathcal{X} = \mathbb{R}^p$ : \[\begin{aligned} d\mu_p(x) &= (2\pi)^{-\frac{n}{2}}e^{-\frac{-|x|^2}{2}} dx \\ \mu_p(A) &= \int_A d\mu_p(x) \end{aligned} \]

    "Aire" de A = probabilitĂ© que $Z \sim \mu_p = \mathcal{N}(0, \mathrm{Id}_p)$ soit dans $A$

  • Fonction isopĂ©rimĂ©trique de $\mu_p$ :

    $$\mathcal{I}_{\mu_p} = \phi \circ \Phi^{-1},$$

    avec $$\phi(x) = \frac{e^{-\frac{x^2}{2}}}{\sqrt{2\pi}} \quad\text{et}\quad \Phi = s \mapsto \int_{-\infty}^{s}\phi(x)dx = \mu_1(]-\infty, s[)$$

3. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique

b. Extension Ă  des surfaces gĂ©nĂ©ralisĂ©es

  • Mesure gaussienne $\mu_p$ dans $\mathcal{X} = \mathbb{R}^p$ : \[\begin{aligned} d\mu_p(x) &= (2\pi)^{-\frac{n}{2}}e^{-\frac{-|x|^2}{2}} dx \\ \mu_p(A) &= \int_A d\mu_p(x) \end{aligned} \]

    "Aire" de A = probabilitĂ© que $Z \sim \mu_p = \mathcal{N}(0, \mathrm{Id}_p)$ soit dans $A$

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

a. Et alors ?

    On a
  • Une mesure engendrĂ©e par la mesure gaussienne "pushed-forward" :
  • Une mesure engendrĂ©e par la mesure gaussienne "pushed-forward" : $$\nu_{\theta} = g_{\theta \#}\mu_p$$
  • Une inĂ©galiĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour la mesure gaussienne : $$\mu_p^{+}(\partial A) \geq (\phi \circ \Phi^{-1})(\mu_p(A))$$

pad

    On veut
  • Une mesure engendrĂ©e par la mesure gaussienne "pushed-forward" :
  • Une inĂ©galitĂ© sur la mesure "pushed-forward" :

    $$\operatorname{Lip}(g_{\theta})(g_{\theta \#}\mu_p)^{+}(\partial A) \geq (\phi \circ \Phi^{-1})(g_{\theta \#}\mu_p(A))$$

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

a. Et alors ?

  • Une mesure engendrĂ©e par la mesure gaussienne "pushed-forward" :
  • Une inĂ©galitĂ© sur la mesure "pushed-forward" :

    $$\operatorname{Lip}(g_{\theta})(g_{\theta \#}\mu_p)^{+}(\partial A) \geq (\phi \circ \Phi^{-1})(g_{\theta \#}\mu_p(A))$$

  • Une mesure engendrĂ©e par la mesure gaussienne "pushed-forward" :
  • S'Ă©crit aussi, pour tout $r \geq 0$ :

    $$(g_{\theta \#}\mu_p)(A_r) \geq \Phi\left(\frac{r}{\operatorname{Lip}(g_{\theta})} + \Phi^{-1}\left(g_{\theta \#}\mu_p(A)\right)\right)$$

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

a. Et alors ?

pad

  • $g_{\theta \#}\mu_p$ multimodal $\Rightarrow$ $\operatorname{Lip}(g_{\theta})$ large

    Mais $\operatorname{Lip}(g_{\theta})$ terme de rĂ©gularisation

Compromis expressivitĂ© - stabilitĂ©

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

b. ExpĂ©rimentations

pad

  • VAE
  • modes gaussiens
  • interpole fortement entre eux
  • GAN
  • mode collapse
  • pas d'interpolation
  • SGM
  • modes gaussiens
  • pas d'interpolation

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

b. ExpĂ©rimentations

pad

  • $\operatorname{Lip}(\operatorname{GAN}) \gg \operatorname{Lip}(\operatorname{VAE})$
  • GAN & VAE saturent la borne infĂ©rieure
  • SGM appelĂ©e plusieurs fois $\Rightarrow$ compromis gagnant

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

b. ExpĂ©rimentations

pad

RĂ©gularisation $\;\operatorname{Lip}(\operatorname{GAN})$

mode dropping $\rightarrow$ mode collapse

4. InĂ©galitĂ© isopĂ©rimĂ©trique pour push-forward models

b. ExpĂ©rimentations

pad

  • InstabilitĂ© croissante avec profondeur et temps d'entraĂ®nement

Multiview registration

of highly degraded point clouds

of highly degraded point clouds

of highly degraded point clouds

ICube Lab | Strasbourg

Luc Vedrenne - Denis Fortun - Sylvain Faisan

Point Clouds

  • Comes from surface sampling
  • Unordered set of cartesian coordinates : $$ x \in \mathbb{R}^{k \times 3}$$
  • Unordered set of cartesian coordinates : $$ x \in \mathbb{R}^{k \times 3}$$
    Applications
  • Autonomous driving
  • Robotics
  • Microscopy
  • List of cartesian coordinates : $$ x \in \mathbb{R}^{k \times 3}$$

Multiview registration

1. Rigid motion

Rigid motion $=$ affine map $\,\rho\,$ of $\,\mathbb{R^3}\,$: $$ \rho x = Rx + t$$

$\rho \in SE(3)$, $\quad R$ rotation matrix $\in S0(3)$, $\quad t$ translation vector $\in \mathbb{R^3}$

2. Pairwise Rigid Registration

Find the rigid motion that best align two points clouds $x$ and $y$

$$ \rho^{\star} = \underset{\rho}{\text{argmin}}\, \lVert \rho x - y \rVert $$

3. Multiview extension

  • Set of $N$ point clouds
  • Find $N$ rigid motions aligning the $N$ point clouds

pad

pad

Two approaches

Two approaches

1. All pairwise registration, then motion synchronization

  • bad scaling with number of views: $\,\mathcal{O}(N^2)$
  • requires robustness to failed pairwise registrations
  • pad

Two approaches

2. Generative modeling

  • better scaling with number of views: $\,\mathcal{O}(N)$
  • robustness
  • harder optimization problem
  • typically : EM-GMM

Degradations

Reference

pad

1. Anisotropic noise

pad

2. Partial visibility

3. Outliers

Real data

Single Molecule Localization Microscopy

C. Franke, "Advancing Single-Molecule Localization Microscopy: Quantitative Analyses and Photometric Three-Dimensional Imaging" (2019)

Why is it hard ?

1. Local correspondences

Zhengyang Shen, Jean Feydy et al. "Accurate Point Cloud Registration with Robust Optimal Transport", NeurIPS 2021

  • unreliable on higly degraded data
  • sensitive to point density
  • sensitive to repetitive patterns

pad

2. Local convergence

2. Local convergence

pad

2. Local convergence

POLAR : POint cloud LAtent Registration

POLAR : POint cloud LAtent Registration

Core idea

  1. Pretrain an autoencoder to reconstruct degraded point clouds
  2. Once trained, freeze it
  3. Registrate within its latent space

Autoencoder

$\mathcal{L} = \lVert x - x' \rVert$

pad

Benefits of registering within the autoencoder's latent space

pad

  1. Generative method
    • template $=$ $d(\hat{z})$ instead of GMM
    • pad
  2. Global descriptor : no local correspondences
    • point cloud discrepancy $=$ $\lVert z_1 - z_2 \rVert$
    • pad
  3. Time efficiency
    • scales well with number of points

pad

POLAR graphical model

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\textcolor{007BFF}{\hat{\rho}_i} d(\textcolor{007BFF}{\hat{z}}) ) - e(x_i) \rVert_{_{_2}}$

Taking degradations into account

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\textcolor{007BFF}{\hat{\rho}_i} d(\textcolor{007BFF}{\hat{z}}) ) - e(x_i) \rVert_{_{_2}}$

  • $d(\hat{z})$ should be a clean object
  • $z_i = e(x_i)$ are latent vectors of degraded object
  • pad

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\hat{\rho}_i d(\hat{z}) ) - e(x_i) \rVert_{_{_2}}$

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\hat{\rho}_i d(\hat{z}) + \textcolor{red}{\varepsilon}) - e(x_i) \rVert_{_{_2}}$

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\textcolor{green}{\operatorname{M}_i^c}(\hat{\rho}_i d(\hat{z}) + \textcolor{red}{\varepsilon})) - e(x_i) \rVert_{_{_2}}$

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\textcolor{green}{\operatorname{M}_i^c}(\hat{\rho}_i d(\hat{z}) + \textcolor{red}{\varepsilon})) - e(\textcolor{blue}{\operatorname{M}_i^o}(x_i)) \rVert_{_{_2}}$

$\textcolor{red}{\text{anisotropic noise}}$

$\textcolor{green}{\text{partial visibility mask}}\hspace{40pt}\textcolor{red}{\text{anisotropic noise}}$

$\textcolor{green}{\text{partial visibility mask}}\hspace{50pt}\textcolor{red}{\text{anisotropic noise}}\hspace{30pt}\textcolor{blue}{\text{outliers mask}}\hspace{60pt}$

$\sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Sigma)$

$\sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Sigma)$

$\sim \mathcal{N}(0, \Sigma)$

pad

Taking degradations into account

Taking degradations into account

pad

pad

pad

Regularization matters

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\operatorname{M}_i(\hat{\rho}_i d(\hat{z}) + \varepsilon_i)) - z_i \rVert_{_{_2}}$

$\mathcal{L} = \sum_i \lVert e(\operatorname{M}_i(\hat{\rho}_i d(\hat{z}) + \varepsilon_i)) - z_i \rVert_{_{_2}} + r(d(\hat{z}))$

standard deviation

of points density

Optimization scheme

  • Find plausible initializations
    • Coarse exhaustive search over $SO(3)$ with fixed $\hat{z}$ and $t$
  • Parallel gradient descent from each initialization
